digest.component_or_area: config discovery / tombstone registry for retired env & TOML inputs
propositions: Retired but still-set env vars and TOML keys are silently ignored or fall through to defaults, redirecting the store or weakening security/lifecycle policy without any operator signal.
A RemovedInput tombstone registry (kind env|cfg|file, name, replacement, removed_in, behavior error|warn) is applied at config discovery and CLI startup before defaults are chosen.

Error-class retired inputs raise a dedicated RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so it is not swallowed by tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError or the MCP boot's broad except Exception handlers.

The env-var and file-presence (.rebar/config.conf) tombstone checks fire at the top of load_config() before the _RESULT_CACHE lookup so they trigger on a warm cache hit in the long-running MCP server, with tombstoned names added to _env_signature().

Retired inputs affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, security, or lifecycle policy hard-error with a message naming old name, replacement, and removed-in release, while operationally inert inputs only warn.

A CI golden-file check (tests/golden/config_surface.json) fails if a recognised config key or env var is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry, and genuinely-unknown keys retain the existing WARN/REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS policy.
